title: ESPuino
restartinfo:
ESPuino is being restarted...
Page reloads automatically after restart finished.
shutdowninfo:
ESPuino is now switched off...
Page reloads automatically when the ESPuino is switched on again.

toast.ledCountRestart: The number of LEDs was changed. ESPuino will restart to apply it.

status.disconnected: Connection to ESPuino lost - trying to reconnect...

led.title: LED settings
led.numidledots: Number of LEDs in idle state
led.numindicatorExp: Number of LEDs used for status/progress indication (e.g. volume, playback progress). A restart is required for the change to take effect.
led.numcontrolExp: Number of extra LEDs at the end of the chain that can each be assigned their own freely chosen color. A restart is required for the change to take effect.
led.numidledotsExp: Number of dots/LEDs shown while idle (no playback active).
led.huestartExp: Hue (0-255) at the start of the progress indicator. -1 = use the default color.
led.hueendExp: Hue (0-255) at the end of the progress indicator. -1 = use the default color.
led.hueatmoExp: Hue (0-255) in ambient light mode. -1 = use the default color.
led.satatmoExp: Saturation (0-255) in ambient light mode. -1 = use the default value.
led.dimmablestatesExp: Number of intermediate steps per LED, used to make transitions (e.g. volume or progress indicator) smoother/finer.
led.offsetExp: Shifts the first LED of the indicator by this many positions - useful if the LED ring shouldn't start at position 0.
led.offsetpauseExp: Offsets the pause indicator by half an LED spacing compared to the normal idle indicator, so pause is visually distinguishable from idle.
led.reverserotationExp: Reverses the direction the LED indicator runs in, e.g. for rings mounted counter-clockwise instead of clockwise.

buttons.title: Button assignments
buttons.titleExp: Assigns each button an action for a short press and one for a long press.

multibuttons.title: Multi-button assignments
multibuttons.titleExp: Triggers an action when two buttons are pressed at the same time.

rotary.title: Rotary encoder
rotary.reverseExp: Reverses the rotary encoder's counting/turning direction, e.g. if volume up/down is swapped.
rotary.gesturesExp: While one of these buttons is held down, turning the encoder triggers the assigned action instead of changing the volume. 'Turn right' is the direction that raises the volume. The long-press action of such a button fires when it is released - and not at all if the encoder was turned.
rotary.seekPreviewTitleExp: Fine-tuning for the 🎯 seek preview gesture (see table above): how long to wait before a preview auto-commits, and how finely the target position can be steered by turning.
rotary.seekPrevDelayExp: How long (in ms) the encoder must stay idle before a 🎯 seek-preview gesture jumps to the previewed position on its own. Releasing the held button always commits immediately, regardless of this delay. 0-3000, default 2000.
rotary.seekPrevSweepExp: How many encoder detents move the seek-preview target from 0% to 100% (blue LED) of the track. Lower = coarser/faster scrubbing, higher = finer control. Default 40.

ssid.title: WiFi-name (SSID)
ssid.explanation: Name of the WiFi network ESPuino should join.

password.title: Password
password.explanation: Password of the WiFi network ESPuino should join.

hostname.title: ESPuino's name (hostname)
hostname.explanation: This is the name of your ESPuino as it appears in your network.

apSSID.title: ESPuino access point name
apSSID.explanation: Name of the WiFi network ESPuino opens when either no WiFi has been configured yet, or no configured WiFi is in range.

apPassword.title: ESPuino access point password
apPassword.explanation: Password of the WiFi network ESPuino opens when either no WiFi has been configured yet, or no configured WiFi is in range.

apTimeout.title: Access point closes after (minutes)
apTimeout.hint: 0 = access point never closes automatically (security risk!)
apTimeout.explanation: Duration in minutes for which the WiFi network ESPuino opens (when either no WiFi has been configured yet, or no configured WiFi is in range) stays open.

static.explanation: IP configuration happens manually instead of via a DHCP server. Caution: you should know what to enter here, otherwise your ESPuino may no longer be reachable over the network.
static.addrExp: Fixed IPv4 address for ESPuino on the WiFi network (only effective if 'Static IP configuration' is enabled).
static.gatewayExp: IP address of the gateway/router for the static IP configuration.
static.subnetExp: Subnet mask for the static IP configuration, e.g. 255.255.255.0.
static.dns1Exp: Preferred DNS server for the static IP configuration.
static.dns2Exp: Alternative DNS server for the static IP configuration (used if the first one is unreachable).

scan.explanation: If multiple WiFi networks are configured in ESPuino, or you're running a mesh WiFi, ESPuino won't just join the first configured network it finds while scanning. Instead, it scans all networks and joins the strongest one. Advantage: higher throughput. Disadvantage: takes a bit longer.

fullInterface.title: Just want the web interface?
fullInterface.hint: If you don't want to set up WiFi but just access the interface (e.g. away from home with no known WiFi): close this window, open your regular browser and go to this address.
fullInterface.copyFailed: Could not copy - please select and copy the address above manually.

fail.wrong_password: Could not join "{{ssid}}" — the password is probably wrong (code {{code}}). Please check it and try again.
fail.not_found: Network "{{ssid}}" was not found (code {{code}}). Is it in range and on 2.4 GHz?
fail.rejected: The router refused the connection to "{{ssid}}" (code {{code}}). Check MAC filters, client limits or security settings (WPA2 required).
fail.timeout: Joined "{{ssid}}", but no IP address was received (code {{code}}). Check the router's DHCP settings.

test.success: Connected! The box got IP {{ip}}. It will now leave setup mode — reconnect your phone to your normal WiFi and reach the box at http://{{ip}}
test.reachHint: Your ESPuino is now on your WiFi at this address. Copy it or save it as a bookmark — the setup network is closing.

directory.title: Directory
directory.desc: Upload directory with all files and subdirectories

upload.title: Upload
upload.selectFile: Please select files or folders to upload!

fileurl.title: File, directory or URL (^ and # aren't allowed as chars)

mode.12: 🎲💤 Single track of a directory (random). Followed by sleep.
mode.15: 🌳 All tracks of a directory + subdirectories (sorted, recursive)
mode.17: 🌳🔀 All tracks of a directory + subdirectories (random, recursive)
mode.7: 📁🔁 All tracks of a directory (sorted, loop)
mode.9: 📁🔀🔁 All tracks of a directory (random, loop)
mode.13: 🎲📁 All tracks of a random subdirectory (sorted)
mode.14: 🎲📁🔀 All tracks of a random subdirectory (random)
mode.11: 📃 List (files from SD and/or webstreams) from local .m3u-File

cmd.107: 💤 Sleep after end of five tracks
cmd.186: 🎯 Seek preview (turn to scrub, commits on release/idle)

mqtt.title: MQTT-settings

clientId.title: MQTT-ClientId
clientId.explanation: Unique identifier ESPuino uses to log in to the MQTT broker - must be unique on the network. The placeholder <MAC> is replaced with the MAC address.

server.title: MQTT-server
server.explanation: Hostname or IP address of the MQTT broker.

baseTopic.title: Base topic (optional)
baseTopic.explanation: Base path under which all of ESPuino's MQTT topics are published, e.g. 'espuino/livingroom/...'.

deviceId.title: Device Id
deviceId.explanation: Device identifier that is part of the MQTT topic path - useful to tell multiple ESPuinos on the same broker apart. The placeholder <MAC> is replaced with the MAC address.

mqtt.restartNote: Note: MQTT settings only take effect after restarting the device.
mqtt.restartPrompt: MQTT settings were saved. Restart now to apply changes?

user.title: MQTT-username (optional)
user.explanation: Username for logging in to the MQTT broker (if it requires authentication).

pwd.title: MQTT-password (optional)
pwd.explanation: Password for logging in to the MQTT broker (if it requires authentication).

port.title: MQTT-port
port.explanation: TCP port of the MQTT broker (default: 1883 for unencrypted connections).

mqtt.enableExp: Enables the connection to an MQTT broker. A restart is required for the change to take effect.

user.title: FTP-Username
user.explanation: Username for accessing ESPuino via FTP.

pwd.title: FTP-password
pwd.explanation: Password for accessing ESPuino via FTP.

start.title: Start FTP-server
start.desc: Enables FTP-server until device is restarted.

normal.title: Exit Bluetooth Mode
normal.desc: Returns to normal mode with WiFi functionality. Music from SD card and web streams can be played.

sink.title: ESPuino as Bluetooth speaker
sink.desc: ESPuino is started as a Bluetooth speaker.

source.title: Connect with Bluetooth headphone
source.desc: The device connects to the specified Bluetooth headset.

scan.title: Scan for devices
scan.notAllowed: Bluetooth device scan not allowed in current mode. Please switch to Bluetooth headphone mode first.

device.title: Bluetooth device (headphone)
device.explanation: Name ESPuino shows during Bluetooth pairing, or uses when connecting as a Bluetooth headphone.

pincode.title: Pairing PIN-Code
pincode.explanation: PIN code for Bluetooth pairing (only needed if the target device asks for it).

switchmode.title: Bluetooth Mode Active
switchmode.prompt: Control via website is restricted in Bluetooth mode. Would you like to switch back to WiFi mode and restart?

volume.title: Volume
volume.restartExp: Volume ESPuino starts with after power-up (unless 'Remember last volume' is enabled).
volume.minExp: Lowest selectable volume. Prevents ESPuino from being muted entirely - e.g. so children cannot accidentally turn it silent. Must be lower than both maximum volumes. Takes effect after a restart.
volume.speakerMaxExp: Maximum volume allowed when playing back through the speaker.
volume.headphoneMaxExp: Maximum volume allowed when playing back through headphones.

equalizer.title: Equalizer
equalizer.info: According to the library (ESP32-audioI2S), the frequencies are 500 Hz LowShelf, 3000 Hz PeakEQ and 6000 Hz HighShelf.

rfid.title: RFID Reader

readerType.title: Reader Type
readerType.description: Select the type of RFID reader connected to your ESPuino. Auto-detect will try to determine the connected reader type automatically.

rfid.pn5180LpcdExp: Enable Low Power Card Detection for PN5180 RFID reader. Only possible if solder-pins are set correctly.
rfid.mfrc522GainExp: Sets the sensitivity of the MFRC522 RFID reader. Possible values are 0 (lowest sensitivity) to 7 (highest sensitivity). Default is 7.
rfid.mfrc522ScanIntervalExp: Time in milliseconds between two polls of the MFRC522 RFID reader. A restart is required for a change to take effect. Default is 100ms.
rfid.pn5180DebounceExp: Time in milliseconds a card must go undetected before it's considered removed. Prevents brief read dropouts (e.g. with a larger gap between card and reader) from being mistaken for card removal. A restart is required for a change to take effect. Default is 500ms.

options.title: Options
options.playMonoExp: If two speakers are installed, stereo can be played. More information here.
options.savePlayPosShutdownExp: When the box is switched off, the playback position for audio books is saved. More information here under SAVE_PLAYPOS_BEFORE_SHUTDOWN.
options.savePlayPosRfidChange: Remember playback position at RFID tag change
options.savePlayPosRfidChangeExp: When switching to a new RFID tag, the playback position is saved for audio books. Does not affect modification tags. More information here under SAVE_PLAYPOS_WHEN_RFID_CHANGE.
options.savePlayPosInterval: Auto-save playback position for long audio books every
options.savePlayPosIntervalExp: Periodically saves the playback position of long audio books (files of 5 minutes or more) to flash while playing, so a sudden power loss (dead battery, unplugged, crash) only rewinds by up to the set number of seconds instead of restarting the file. Only applies to audio-book playmodes; short or split tracks already save at each track boundary. A shorter interval writes to flash more often, so 30-60 seconds is recommended; going much lower gains little and wears the flash faster.
options.pauseIfRfidRemoved: Pause when RFID tag is removed
options.pauseIfRfidRemovedExp: Playback is paused when the RFID tag is removed.
options.dontAcceptRfidTwice: Don't accept the same RFID twice
options.dontAcceptRfidTwiceExp: RFID tags are not accepted twice in a row. Cannot be used together with Pause when RFID tag is removed.
options.resumeOnSameRfid: Resume from pause when placing the same RFID again
options.resumeOnSameRfidExp: If playback is paused and the same RFID tag is placed again, playback continues. Only effective if Don't accept the same RFID twice is enabled.
options.playLastRfidOnReboot: Play last RFID tag on restart
options.playLastRfidOnRebootExp: Play last RFID tag on restart.
options.pauseOnMinVolumeExp: Playback is paused when the volume reaches the minimum level. More information here
options.recoverVolBootExp: After a restart, the speaker remembers which volume was last used and automatically adjusts it again.
options.volumeCurveExp: Use of logarithmic volume calculation. Can help if the gradations in the lower volume range are too coarse.

neopixel.title: Neopixel (brightness)
neopixel.nightmodeExp: Reduced LED brightness in night mode.
neopixel.atmomodeExp: LED brightness in ambient light mode (Atmolight).

sleep.title: Deep Sleep
sleep.inactivityExp: Time in minutes without any interaction after which ESPuino automatically goes to sleep (deep sleep).

battery.title: Battery
battery.lowWarning: Show warning below this threshold (in volt)
battery.lowCritical: Lowest voltage (in volt), that is indicated by one LED
battery.criticalShutoff: Below this voltage (in volt), ESPuino turns off
battery.high: Voltage (in volt), that is indicated by all LEDs
battery.shutdownOnCritical: Automatically shut down on critical voltage
battery.lowWarningExp: Voltage below which a visual (LED) low-battery warning is triggered.
battery.lowCriticalExp: Voltage that is shown as 0% battery charge (empty battery).
battery.highExp: Voltage that is shown as 100% battery charge (full battery).
battery.criticalShutoffExp: Voltage at which ESPuino automatically shuts down to protect the battery from deep discharge.
battery.measureIntervalExp: Time interval in minutes at which the battery voltage is measured.
battery.shutdownOnCriticalExp: Automatically shuts ESPuino down once the battery voltage drops below the critical threshold set below. Can help protect the battery from deep discharge to some extent, but does not replace a battery management system! Recommended values are: LFP: 2.7 V, LiPo: 3.0 V.

playlist.title: Playlist
playlist.sortMode: Sorting mode for playlist and file browser
playlist.recDepth: Maximum recursion depth at generation of recursive playlists
playlist.sortModeExp: Determines how files and playlists are sorted: standard alphabetical sorting (e.g. '10' before '2') or natural sorting, which orders numbers in names correctly (e.g. '2' before '10'), optionally case-sensitive.
playlist.recDepthExp: Maximum directory depth to search when generating recursive playlists from subfolders.

nvs.title: Saved RFID assignments
nvs.desc: The RFID assignments are not stored on the SD card but on the internal, non-volative storage (NVS) of the ESPuino.

list.title: List of all stored RFID assignments

erase.title: Erase all RFID assignments
erase.desc:
Via importer new entries will only be inserted but old ones won't be erased. Only in case an old assignment to the same entry already exists, it will be overwritten. With this function all existing RFID assignments will be erased.
Further information (in German).

import.title: Import
import.desc: A backup file can be uploaded here in order to import RFID assignments.

export.title: Export
export.desc: A backup file can be downloaded here in order to export the RFID assignments.

fwupdate.title: Firmware-Update
fwupdate.desc: Firmware can be updated right here.

github.title: Load firmware from GitHub
github.desc: Downloads a ready-built firmware directly from the ESPuino-Firmware repository and installs it – no manual download needed.
github.noBuilds: No builds found for this selection.
github.notAvailablePlatform: No prebuilt firmwares are provided in the repository for this platform ({{platform}}). Please upload the firmware manually.
github.notAvailableCustomBuild: This board was built with custom settings (settings-override.h / platformio-override.ini). The official firmware does not include these customizations – please build it yourself and upload it manually as usual.
github.notAvailableOta: This board does not support firmware updates via the web interface. Please flash the firmware over serial.
github.fetchError: Could not fetch the firmware list.

forum.title: Forum
forum.desc:
Having problems or aim to discuss about ESPuino?
Join us at ESPuino-Forum! Especially there's a lot of (german) documentation online!

swagger.title: Swagger
swagger.desc: Documentation of the REST API: Swagger.

systeminfo.title: Information
systeminfo.hallsensor: HallEffectSensor NullFieldValue: {{hsnullfieldvalue}}, actual: {{hsactual}}, diff:{{hsdiff}}, LastWaitFor_State:{{hslastwaitforstate}} (waited:{{hswaited}} ms)
